acceptance_targets: [ch05-f004] PASS if a cold read of the revised 195-227 span can attribute at least one untagged line each to Tate, Clay, and Weems by diction/rhythm alone, and at least one leverage shift is clear in the exchange; FAIL if voices remain interchangeable.

[ch05-f005] PASS if Beau and Mara use contractions in at least two of the three targeted beats and any retained full-form phrase is clearly motivated by emphasis or social performance in-context; FAIL if the lines stay stiff by default.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a cold read of the revised 195-227 span can attribute at least one untagged line each to Tate, Clay, and Weems by diction/rhythm alone, and at least one leverage shift is clear in the exchange; FAIL if voices remain interchangeable.
findings.problem: Tate, Clay, and Weems converge into similar polished bureaucratic cadence in the hearing. The exchange carries conflict, but idiolect separation is too thin for a high-pressure scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework speaker lines in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:195-227 so each voice has a distinct syntax and leverage style: Tate as managerial triage, Clay as falsely courteous institutional control, Weems as legalistic risk framing.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Beau and Mara use contractions in at least two of the three targeted beats and any retained full-form phrase is clearly motivated by emphasis or social performance in-context; FAIL if the lines stay stiff by default.
findings.problem: Contraction register drifts stiff in spots for high-contraction characters (Beau, Mara) without a clear formal-performance trigger, creating brief over-formality against the book default.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust the targeted lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:501 to contracted, pressure-natural speech unless deliberate emphasis is explicitly signaled.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
